name: min_chars-inclusive-boundary
note: Input is exactly 10 bytes; min_chars:10 is inclusive (>=), so it matches.

name: max_chars-inclusive-boundary
note: Input is exactly 6 bytes; max_chars:6 is inclusive (<=), so it matches.

name: between-bounds-default
note: 7 bytes is above max_chars:6 and below min_chars:10, so no rule matches and it falls through to default.

name: max_chars-utf8-byte-count
note: Each 'é' is 1 character but 2 UTF-8 bytes, so 'éééé' is 4 characters / 8 bytes. 8 bytes misses max_chars:6 -> default (local); counting characters (4 <= 6) would match -> cloud. So routing to local proves length is measured in bytes, not characters.
